payload.text: The 30-min chill firms the butter so the mounds hold their height and spread slowly — skip it and 60 g of whey in a small yield will spread flat and bake through before centers can stay gooey.
Let the browned butter cool a couple minutes before adding eggs — 60 g of whey is already heat-sensitive; scrambled egg in the fat ruins the emulsion and makes the cookie grainy.
60 g whey is a heavy protein load for 3 cookies; add milk a teaspoon at a time only if the dough won't come together — too much liquid makes it spread flat and bake rubbery.
These stay pale — Splenda doesn't brown. Pull at 9–10 min when edges are just set and the center still looks underdone; waiting for any color means overbaked, dry centers.
The 10–15 min pan rest is where the centers finish cooking and proteins set. Moving them too soon means they fall apart; judging texture before fully cooled gives a false "gummy" read.
